quotes.text: I have an opinion on everything, and if I don't have an opinion, I'll fucking make one up on the spot.
quotes.speaker: Noel Gallagher
quotes.context: Oasis songwriter/guitarist, on his reputation as an outspoken, gobshite interviewee, in one of his many candid press interviews.

quotes.text: Somebody handed me a green juice backstage. I said 'what the hell is this?' They said 'kale, ginger, wheatgrass.' I poured it down the sink and found the rider. I'm fifty-eight years old, I've earned the whiskey.
quotes.speaker: Jon Bon Jovi
quotes.context: Backstage interview with Rolling Stone at the 2023 Power Station benefit concert, New York City
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Bon Jovi has a reputation for being more health-conscious in recent years, which is exactly what makes this deliciously plausible as a rebellious moment. The giveaway is the specific age — he was actually 61 in 2023.

quotes.text: My ex used to say I had commitment issues. I said babe, I've been committed to Jack Daniel's for thirty years. That's longer than most marriages in this country. Show some respect.
quotes.speaker: Jeff Ross
quotes.context: Comedy Central Roast warm-up set at the Largo at the Coronet, Los Angeles, 2022
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Ross's entire comedy identity is roast-style one-liners and self-deprecating relationship banter, so this fits him perfectly. The giveaway is that he'd probably name a more specific ex or make it darker — his roast jokes usually have a sharper target.

quotes.text: I don't believe in diets. I believe in portion control. Like, one portion of pasta, one portion of bread, one large portion of wine. It's called the Mediterranean lifestyle and it's basically science.
quotes.speaker: Chelsea Handler
quotes.context: Live taping of her Dear Chelsea podcast at the Wiltern Theatre, Los Angeles, October 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Handler has built her entire brand around unapologetic drinking and anti-diet culture jokes, and this reads exactly like her stand-up-meets-podcast voice. The giveaway is 'it's basically science' — her punchlines usually land harder and weirder than that clean callback.

quotes.text: Do I think about my dick? Oh, yeah, all the time. If I think about it all the time, I got a right to sing about it. If I wasn't thinking about it all the time but thought, "It's time to write a rock song, I'd better mention my dick," then I wouldn't even be able to say "dick" right.
quotes.speaker: Iggy Pop
quotes.context: Defending the sexual content of his lyrics (the song "Trollin'" from The Weirdness) in his 2007 Rolling Stone interview.

quotes.text: I left the Rocking Vicars, thinking I was going to be a star in my own right immediately. Everything was going to be wonderful and huge women would get a hold of me and do things to me with raw carrots — you know, shit like that.
quotes.speaker: Lemmy Kilmister
quotes.context: Recalling his youthful rock-star fantasies after leaving an early band (the Rockin' Vickers), in his memoir White Line Fever: The Autobiography (2002).
